reasoning:
FIDE releases official chess ratings once a month, typically on the 1st. The Wikipedia FIDE rankings page mirrors these monthly lists. 
1) On March 8, 2026, the Wikipedia page will almost certainly display the exact same rating list as on March 1, 2026. Therefore, her rating on March 8 will be identical (0% increase) to March 1.
2) On March 31, 2026, the page will likely still display the March list. Even if the April 1st list is updated a day early, Humpy's rating will not have increased. A 1% increase requires gaining over 25 Elo points. Humpy is competing in the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament, which begins on March 28, 2026. Elite players virtually never play classical games in the immediate month prior to the Candidates, to focus on preparation. Consequently, she will have no classical games rated for the April list. Even if the few initial rounds of the Candidates somehow managed to be rated for April (which violates FIDE's policy of rating tournaments only after they conclude), 2-3 games mathematically cannot alter her rating by 25 points.
Given the mechanics of FIDE's rating periods and Humpy's schedule, it is nearly impossible for her rating to be 1% higher on either date.
